summary: Austrian Symbolist painter and founding member of the Vienna Secession. Known for The Kiss (gold leaf), Beethoven Frieze, Portrait of Adele Bloch-Bauer (Woman in Gold), and Danae. Characteristic 'golden phase' using gold leaf.

works.name: The Kiss (1907-1908)
works.description: Couple in golden robes embracing on a flowered cliff. Man's garment has rectangles, woman's has circles. Characteristic gold leaf. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Couple in golden robes embracing on a flowered cliff; man has rectangles, woman has circles; gold leaf
examples: This artist's best-known painting depicts a couple dressed in yellow robes embracing, and features his characteristic use of gold leaf
A cliff covered in flowers supports a woman covered in circles and a man with rectangles kneeling and embracing

works.name: Beethoven Frieze (1902)
works.description: Three-wall frieze in the Secession Building depicting Beethoven's Ninth Symphony / Ode to Joy. Features Typhoeus, three Gorgons, floating Genii, and a knight in gold armor. 7 feet tall, 112 feet wide. Created for the 14th Vienna Secessionist exhibition alongside Max Klinger's Beethoven sculpture. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Three-wall frieze in Vienna Secession Building depicting themes of Beethoven's Ninth / Ode to Joy
examples: Name this painting that covers three walls in the Secession Building in Vienna
this artist of the Beethoven Frieze, the leading painter of the Vienna Secession

clues.clue: Features ape-like giant Typhoeus and his three Gorgon daughters as hostile forces
examples: hostile forces like the monkey-like giant Typhoeus and his daughters, the Gorgons

works.name: Portrait of Adele Bloch-Bauer I (1907)
works.description: Portrait in golden dress decorated with eyes. Also called 'Woman in Gold.' Briefly most expensive painting ever ($135M, 2006). Stolen by Nazis; reclaimed by Maria Altmann. Now at Neue Galerie. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Portrait of Viennese salon lady in golden dress decorated with eyes; exemplifies gold phase
examples: This artist's Pallas Athene makes use of his trademark gold leaf, which is abundant in his portrait of an industrialist's wife

clues.clue: Stolen by Nazis; reclaimed by Maria Altmann in Republic of Austria vs. Altmann
examples: Nazis stole five paintings by this artist that were successfully reclaimed by Maria Altmann

clues.clue: Briefly most expensive painting ever at $135 million in 2006; now at Neue Galerie
examples: which was briefly the most expensive painting ever when it sold for $135 million in 2006

examples: Klimt's second painting of this woman is on display at the MoMa

works.name: Danae (1907-1908)
works.description: Nude red-haired woman curled in fetal position, surrounded by purple, impregnated by golden rain of Zeus. Only mythological subject other than Pallas Athena.

clues.clue: Nude red-haired woman curled in fetal position, impregnated by golden rain of Zeus
examples: sleeping naked girl with red hair curled up and surrounded by purple being visited and impregnated by the golden rain of Zeus

clues.clue: Only mythological subject other than Pallas Athena; possibly same model as The Kiss
examples: it is his only mythological subject other than 'Pallas Athena'

works.name: Stoclet Frieze / Palais Stoclet
works.description: Mosaic frieze for the Palais Stoclet in Brussels featuring Tree of Life with spiral motif, plus The Expectation and The Embrace. Building designed by Josef Hoffmann.

clues.clue: Frieze for Palais Stoclet in Brussels with Tree of Life (spiral motif), The Expectation, and The Embrace
examples: Three panels titled The Knight, The Expectation, and The Embrace comprise a triptych by this artist which includes a swirling depiction of the Tree of Life

clues.clue: Palais Stoclet is the most famous work of Secessionist architect Josef Hoffmann
examples: This building is the most famous work of Secessionist architect Josef Hoffmann

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Founding member of Vienna Secession. Egon Schiele and Oskar Kokoschka were students. University of Vienna ceiling paintings (Philosophy, Medicine, Jurisprudence) destroyed. Schubert at the Piano destroyed by fire. Painted landscapes of Lake Attersee. Judith, Hope I/II, Pallas Athene, Three Ages of Woman, Birch Forest, The Park. Ver Sacrum magazine.

comprehensive_summary:
Gustav Klimt was an Austrian Symbolist painter and the founding member of the Vienna Secession, most commonly identified by The Kiss and the Beethoven Frieze. His 'golden phase' featured extensive use of gold leaf.

The Kiss depicts a couple in golden robes embracing on a flowered cliff, with the man's garment decorated with rectangles and the woman's with circles. The Beethoven Frieze is a massive work (7 feet tall, 112 feet wide) covering three walls of the Secession Building, depicting Beethoven's Ninth Symphony. It features the ape-like giant Typhoeus and his three Gorgon daughters as hostile forces, floating Genii, and a knight in gold armor. It was created for the 14th Vienna Secessionist exhibition alongside Max Klinger's bare-chested Beethoven sculpture.

Klimt painted two portraits of Adele Bloch-Bauer. The first (1907), also called 'Woman in Gold,' exemplifies his gold phase with a dress decorated with eyes. It was stolen by the Nazis and reclaimed by Maria Altmann in Republic of Austria vs. Altmann, briefly selling for $135 million (then a record). It is now at the Neue Galerie; the second portrait is at MoMA. His Danae shows a nude red-haired woman curled in the fetal position, impregnated by Zeus's golden rain, and is his only mythological subject besides Pallas Athene.

The Stoclet Frieze for the Palais Stoclet in Brussels (Josef Hoffmann's most famous building) features the Tree of Life with spiral motifs and panels The Expectation and The Embrace. His University of Vienna ceiling paintings (Philosophy, Medicine, Jurisprudence) caused scandal and were destroyed by fire at Schloss Immendorf. Schubert at the Piano, commissioned by Nikolaus Dumba, was also destroyed.

Klimt mentored Egon Schiele and Oskar Kokoschka. He painted square-shaped landscapes of Lake Attersee, Birch Forest, The Park (canopy of colored dots), Hope I and II (pregnant model Herma), Judith (Adele Bloch-Bauer as model), Pallas Athene, Three Ages of Woman, and a portrait of partner Emilie Floge. Ver Sacrum was the Secession's magazine. The Secession Building was designed by Joseph Maria Olbrich, nicknamed the 'Golden Cabbage,' with owl decorations by Koloman Moser. Roses Under the Trees was restituted to the Stiasny family in 2022.
